zh.lifeSummary: 當Guido di Monforte於1242年出生在英國瓦維其可爾庫頓時，他的父親Simon V de Montfort34歲，母親Eleanor of England28歲。他和Margherita Aldobrandeschi至少有2個女兒。他去世於1292年在義大利西西利亞梅西那享年50歲。

nameMeanings.name: di
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mandarin form of the surname 狄, a term referring to the tribes in ancient northern China: (i) from Di (狄), the name of an ancient state (thought to have been located in Hebei or Shandong province), which was granted to Xiao Bo or Kao Bo by King Cheng of Zhou (reigned 1042–1021  BC ). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Ku Di (庫狄) family during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) traced back to other minority ethnic groups in ancient northwestern China.

Mandarin form of the Chinese surname 邸, meaning ‘official residence’ in Chinese: (i) from placename Di (邸), the name of a county (unidentified location). (ii) said to be borne by some descendants of Kujula Kadphises (called 丘就郤 or 邸就郤 in Chinese, 30–c. 80 AD ), founder of the Kushan Empire (one branch of the Yuezhi people originating from western China) located in Central Asia during the Eastern Han dynasty (25–220 AD ).

variants.name: Chi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Dong, Yun, Cheng, Jae, Yong, Cheng Chung, Chi, Chun, Duk, Fang, Feng.  Korean Chung, Chong, Jae Hong, Moon, Myong, Pong, Shen, Yoo, Byung, Chang, Chol, Chong Hun.  Southeast Asian Phi, Hung, Ly, Tu, Denh, Do Van, Duong, Hao, Kieu, Mui, Nga, Phat.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 池, meaning ‘moat’ in ancient Chinese: (i) acquired as a surname by people who lived beside a moat. (ii) from Chi (池), said to be the name of an ancient state (of unknown location and period). (iii) from the personal name Chi (池), personal name of a son of an official in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 遲, meaning ‘late’ in Chinese: (i) borne by descendants of Chi Ren (遲任), a person who lived during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the Chinese compound surname Yu-Chi (尉遲), originally the name of a tribe from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iii) from the second element of the personal name Zi Chi (子遲), style name of Fan Xu, a student of Confucius (551–479  BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Monforte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Carmela, Carmine, Rocco, Rosaria, Sal.  Spanish Carlos, Francisco, Luis, Medardo.

Italian and Spanish: habitational name from any of numerous places called Monforte, from Latin mons ‘mountain’ (genitive montis) + fortis ‘strong’, probably a reference to the strategic position of these locations.
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Montfort  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain.

variants.name: Montfort
meanings: French and Breton: variant of   Monfort   1. This surname is most common in Brittany and Haute-Savoie. See also 2 below.
Altered form of Dutch Montfoort: habitational name from Montfoort in Utrecht. It is a cognate of 1 above (see Monfort 1) and also its altered form (or perhaps vice versa).
English (of Norman origin): very rare variant of   Mountford  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Guido
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Guido di Monforte was born in 1242, in Coughton, Warwickshire, England, his father, Simon V de Montfort, was 34 and his mother, Eleanor of England, was 28. He had at least 2 daughters with Margherita Aldobrandeschi. He died in 1292, in Messina, Sicily, Italy, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Guido di Monforte est né en 1242, à Coughton, Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, Simon V de Montfort, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Eleanor of England, avait 28 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 filles avec Margherita Aldobrandeschi. Il est décédé en 1292, à Messina, Sicile, Italie, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.Palace of Westminster, Westminster, Midd: Palace of Westminster, Westminster, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Guido di Monforte 1242 in Coughton, Warwickshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Simon V de Montfort 34 und seine Mutter Eleanor of England 28. Er hatte mit Margherita Aldobrandeschi mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb 1292 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Messina, Sizilien, Italien.

Lui avuto almeno 2 figlie con Margherita Aldobrandeschi. Morì nel 1292, nel Messina, Sicilia, Italia, all'età di 50 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Guido di Monforte(이)가 1242년 영국 워윅실르 코우그h턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Simon V de Montfort(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Eleanor of England(은)는 28세였다. 그는 Margherita Aldobrandeschi(와)과 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1292년 이탈리아 시실리야 메시나에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Palace of Westminster, Westminster, Midd: палеис оф вестминстир, вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Guido di Monforte родился в 1242 г. в местности коугтон, варвиккшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Simon V de Montfort было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Eleanor of England, 28. У него было по крайней мере 2 дочери от Margherita Aldobrandeschi. Он умер в 1292 г. в местности мессина, сисиля, Италия в возрасте 50 лет.
